## Report: Prisma Generator Protocol and DMMF Structure

Based on my exploration of the Prisma monorepo, here's the complete guide to the generator protocol and DMMF structure:

---

### **1. Core Interfaces**

#### **GeneratorOptions** (what generators receive)
**Location:** `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`

```typescript
export type GeneratorOptions = {
  generator: GeneratorConfig
  otherGenerators: GeneratorConfig[]
  schemaPath: string
  dmmf: DMMF.Document
  datasources: DataSource[]
  datamodel: string
  version: string // version hash
  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
  noHints?: boolean
  allowNoModels?: boolean
  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
}
```

#### **GeneratorConfig** (generator configuration from schema)
**Location:** `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`

```typescript
export interface GeneratorConfig {
  name: string
  output: EnvValue | null
  isCustomOutput?: boolean
  provider: EnvValue
  config: {
    output?: never
    provider?: never
    binaryTargets?: never
    previewFeatures?: never
  } & {
    [key: string]: string | string[] | undefined
  }
  binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
  previewFeatures: string[]
  sourceFilePath: string
}

export interface EnvValue {
  fromEnvVar: null | string
  value: null | string
}

export interface BinaryTargetsEnvValue {
  fromEnvVar: string | null
  value: string
  native?: boolean
}

export interface DataSource {
  name: string
  provider: ConnectorType
  activeProvider: ActiveConnectorType
  schemas: string[] | []
  sourceFilePath: string
}

export type BinaryPaths = {
  schemaEngine?: { [binaryTarget: string]: string }
}
```

#### **GeneratorManifest** (generator metadata)
**Location:** `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`

```typescript
export type GeneratorManifest = {
  prettyName?: string
  defaultOutput?: string
  denylists?: {
    models?: string[]
    fields?: string[]
  }
  requiresGenerators?: string[]
  requiresEngines?: EngineType[]
  version?: string
  requiresEngineVersion?: string
}
```

---

### **2. DMMF (Data Model Meta Format) Structure**

#### **DMMF.Document** (root DMMF object)
**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}>
```

#### **DMMF.Datamodel** (contains models, enums, types)
**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}>
```

#### **DMMF.Model** (a single Prisma model)
**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}>

export type PrimaryKey = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string | null
  fields: string[]
}>

export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: string[]
}>
```

#### **DMMF.Field** (a field in a model)
**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean
  type: string  // e.g., "String", "Int", "$ModelName"
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null  // e.g., ['VarChar', ['191']]
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}>

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldDefault = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: Array<string | number>
}>

export type FieldDefaultScalar = string | boolean | number
```

#### **DMMF.Schema** (GraphQL-like schema)
**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export type Schema = ReadonlyDeep<{
  rootQueryType?: string
  rootMutationType?: string
  inputObjectTypes: {
    model?: InputType[]
    prisma?: InputType[]
  }
  outputObjectTypes: {
    model: OutputType[]
    prisma: OutputType[]
  }
  enumTypes: {
    model?: SchemaEnum[]
    prisma: SchemaEnum[]
  }
  fieldRefTypes: {
    prisma?: FieldRefType[]
  }
}>
```

#### **DMMF.Mappings** (operation mappings)
**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}>

export type ModelMapping = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  plural: string
  findUnique?: string | null
  findUniqueOrThrow?: string | null
  findFirst?: string | null
  findFirstOrThrow?: string | null
  findMany?: string | null
  create?: string | null
  createMany?: string | null
  createManyAndReturn?: string | null
  update?: string | null
  updateMany?: string | null
  updateManyAndReturn?: string | null
  upsert?: string | null
  delete?: string | null
  deleteMany?: string | null
  aggregate?: string | null
  groupBy?: string | null
  count?: string | null
  findRaw?: string | null
  aggregateRaw?: string | null
}>
```

#### **DMMF.Enum**
**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export type DatamodelEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]
  dbName?: string | null
  documentation?: string
}>

export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
}>
```

---

### **3. Generator Handler Protocol**

#### **generatorHandler** (main entry point)
**Location:** `/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`

```typescript
export interface Handler {
  onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>
  onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest | Promise<GeneratorManifest>
}

export function generatorHandler(handler: Handler): void
```

**How it works:**
- Reads JSON-RPC 2.0 messages line-by-line from stdin
- Supports two methods: `generate` and `getManifest`
- Writes responses to stderr
- Uses internal JSON-RPC protocol for communication

In a `schema.prisma` file, generators are declared like this:

```prisma
generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client"
  output   = "../src/generated"
  // any custom config options
}
```

**Location example:** `/sandbox/basic-sqlite/prisma/schema.prisma`

### **7. GeneratorProcess (CLI-side Runner)**

**Location:** `/packages/generator-helper/src/GeneratorProcess.ts`

The Prisma CLI uses `GeneratorProcess` to spawn and communicate with generators:

```typescript
export class GeneratorProcess {
  constructor(pathOrCommand: string, options?: GeneratorProcessOptions)
  
  async init(): Promise<void>
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest | null>

}

  data?: any
}
```

```typescript
}
```

All DMMF types are wrapped in `ReadonlyDeep` to ensure immutability.

|---------|-------------|----------|
| `@prisma/generator` | `GeneratorOptions`, `GeneratorConfig`, `GeneratorManifest`, `Generator` | `/packages/generator/src/` |
| `@prisma/generator-helper` | `generatorHandler`, `GeneratorProcess`, `GeneratorError` | `/packages/generator-helper/src/` |
| `@prisma/dmmf` | `DMMF.Document`, `DMMF.Model`, `DMMF.Field`, etc. | `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts` |
| `@prisma/client-generator-js` | `PrismaClientJsGenerator` | `/packages/client-generator-js/src/` |
| `@prisma/client-generator-ts` | `PrismaClientTsGenerator` | `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/` |

---

### **10. Flow Diagram**

```
Prisma CLI
    ↓
GeneratorProcess spawns generator process
    ↓ (JSON-RPC over stdin/stderr)
generatorHandler() reads messages
    ↓
Calls handler.onManifest(config) or handler.onGenerate(options)
    ↓ (contains full DMMF.Document)
Generator produces output files
```

All types are **read-only** and immutable by design, enforced via the `ReadonlyDeep` utility type.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report with file paths and exact TypeScript interfaces.

## Report: Prisma Generator Protocol and DMMF Structure

Based on my exploration of the Prisma monorepo, here's the complete guide to the generator protocol and DMMF structure:

---

### **1. Core Interfaces**

#### **GeneratorOptions** (what generators receive)
**Location:** `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`

```typescript
export type GeneratorOptions = {
  generator: GeneratorConfig
  otherGenerators: GeneratorConfig[]
  schemaPath: string
  dmmf: DMMF.Document
  datasources: DataSource[]
  datamodel: string
  version: string // version hash
  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
  noHints?: boolean
  allowNoModels?: boolean
  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
}
```

#### **GeneratorConfig** (generator configuration from schema)
**Location:** `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`

```typescript
export interface GeneratorConfig {
  name: string
  output: EnvValue | null
  isCustomOutput?: boolean
  provider: EnvValue
  config: {
    output?: never
    provider?: never
    binaryTargets?: never
    previewFeatures?: never
  } & {
    [key: string]: string | string[] | undefined
  }
  binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
  previewFeatures: string[]
  sourceFilePath: string
}

export interface EnvValue {
  fromEnvVar: null | string
  value: null | string
}

export interface BinaryTargetsEnvValue {
  fromEnvVar: string | null
  value: string
  native?: boolean
}

export interface DataSource {
  name: string
  provider: ConnectorType
  activeProvider: ActiveConnectorType
  schemas: string[] | []
  sourceFilePath: string
}

export type BinaryPaths = {
  schemaEngine?: { [binaryTarget: string]: string }
}
```

#### **GeneratorManifest** (generator metadata)
**Location:** `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`

```typescript
export type GeneratorManifest = {
  prettyName?: string
  defaultOutput?: string
  denylists?: {
    models?: string[]
    fields?: string[]
  }
  requiresGenerators?: string[]
  requiresEngines?: EngineType[]
  version?: string
  requiresEngineVersion?: string
}
```

---

### **2. DMMF (Data Model Meta Format) Structure**

#### **DMMF.Document** (root DMMF object)
**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}>
```

#### **DMMF.Datamodel** (contains models, enums, types)
**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}>
```

#### **DMMF.Model** (a single Prisma model)
**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}>

export type PrimaryKey = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string | null
  fields: string[]
}>

export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: string[]
}>
```

#### **DMMF.Field** (a field in a model)
**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean
  type: string  // e.g., "String", "Int", "$ModelName"
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null  // e.g., ['VarChar', ['191']]
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}>

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldDefault = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: Array<string | number>
}>

export type FieldDefaultScalar = string | boolean | number
```

#### **DMMF.Schema** (GraphQL-like schema)
**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export type Schema = ReadonlyDeep<{
  rootQueryType?: string
  rootMutationType?: string
  inputObjectTypes: {
    model?: InputType[]
    prisma?: InputType[]
  }
  outputObjectTypes: {
    model: OutputType[]
    prisma: OutputType[]
  }
  enumTypes: {
    model?: SchemaEnum[]
    prisma: SchemaEnum[]
  }
  fieldRefTypes: {
    prisma?: FieldRefType[]
  }
}>
```

#### **DMMF.Mappings** (operation mappings)
**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}>

export type ModelMapping = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  plural: string
  findUnique?: string | null
  findUniqueOrThrow?: string | null
  findFirst?: string | null
  findFirstOrThrow?: string | null
  findMany?: string | null
  create?: string | null
  createMany?: string | null
  createManyAndReturn?: string | null
  update?: string | null
  updateMany?: string | null
  updateManyAndReturn?: string | null
  upsert?: string | null
  delete?: string | null
  deleteMany?: string | null
  aggregate?: string | null
  groupBy?: string | null
  count?: string | null
  findRaw?: string | null
  aggregateRaw?: string | null
}>
```

#### **DMMF.Enum**
**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export type DatamodelEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]
  dbName?: string | null
  documentation?: string
}>

export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
}>
```

---

### **3. Generator Handler Protocol**

#### **generatorHandler** (main entry point)
**Location:** `/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`

```typescript
export interface Handler {
  onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>
  onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest | Promise<GeneratorManifest>
}

export function generatorHandler(handler: Handler): void
```

**How it works:**
- Reads JSON-RPC 2.0 messages line-by-line from stdin
- Supports two methods: `generate` and `getManifest`
- Writes responses to stderr
- Uses internal JSON-RPC protocol for communication

---

### **4. Generator Schema Declaration**

In a `schema.prisma` file, generators are declared like this:

```prisma
generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client"
  output   = "../src/generated"
  // any custom config options
}
```

**Location example:** `/sandbox/basic-sqlite/prisma/schema.prisma`

---

### **5. Concrete Generator Implementation Examples**

#### **PrismaClientJsGenerator**
**Location:** `/packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts`

```typescript
export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
  
  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}
```

#### **PrismaClientTsGenerator**
**Location:** `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/generator.ts`

```typescript
export class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'
  
  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}
```

#### **Minimal Generator Example**
**Location:** `/packages/generator-helper/src/__tests__/minimal-executable`

```typescript
#!/usr/bin/env node

const { generatorHandler } = require('@prisma/generator-helper')

generatorHandler({
  async onGenerate(options) {
    // options contains: generator, dmmf, datasources, datamodel, etc.
    // Write generated files here
  },
  onManifest(config) {
    return {
      defaultOutput: 'default-output',
      prettyName: 'My Generator',
      requiresEngines: ['schema-engine'],
    }
  },
})
```

---

### **6. JSON-RPC Communication Layer**

**Location:** `/packages/generator-helper/src/json-rpc.ts`

```typescript
export type Request = {
  jsonrpc: '2.0'
  method: string
  params?: any
  id: number
}

export type Response = SuccessResponse | ErrorResponse

export type SuccessResponse = {
  jsonrpc: '2.0'
  result: any
  id: number
}

export type ErrorResponse = {
  jsonrpc: '2.0'
  error: {
    code: number
    message: string
    data: any
  }
  id: number
}
```

---

### **7. GeneratorProcess (CLI-side Runner)**

**Location:** `/packages/generator-helper/src/GeneratorProcess.ts`

The Prisma CLI uses `GeneratorProcess` to spawn and communicate with generators:

```typescript
export class GeneratorProcess {
  constructor(pathOrCommand: string, options?: GeneratorProcessOptions)
  
  async init(): Promise<void>
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest | null>
  stop(): void
}

export class GeneratorError extends Error {
  code?: number
  data?: any
}
```

---

### **8. Key Type Utilities**

**Location:** `/packages/dmmf/src/util.ts`

```typescript
export type ReadonlyDeep<O> = {
  +readonly [K in keyof O]: ReadonlyDeep<O[K]>
}
```

All DMMF types are wrapped in `ReadonlyDeep` to ensure immutability.

---

### **9. Package Exports Summary**

| Package | Main Exports | Location |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| `@prisma/generator` | `GeneratorOptions`, `GeneratorConfig`, `GeneratorManifest`, `Generator` | `/packages/generator/src/` |
| `@prisma/generator-helper` | `generatorHandler`, `GeneratorProcess`, `GeneratorError` | `/packages/generator-helper/src/` |
| `@prisma/dmmf` | `DMMF.Document`, `DMMF.Model`, `DMMF.Field`, etc. | `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts` |
| `@prisma/client-generator-js` | `PrismaClientJsGenerator` | `/packages/client-generator-js/src/` |
| `@prisma/client-generator-ts` | `PrismaClientTsGenerator` | `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/` |

---

### **10. Flow Diagram**

```
Prisma CLI
    ↓
GeneratorProcess spawns generator process
    ↓ (JSON-RPC over stdin/stderr)
generatorHandler() reads messages
    ↓
Calls handler.onManifest(config) or handler.onGenerate(options)
    ↓ (contains full DMMF.Document)
Generator produces output files
```

All types are **read-only** and immutable by design, enforced via the `ReadonlyDeep` utility type.

input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a new Prisma generator package called `@prisma/client-generator-rest-api` that generates a typed REST API client from a Prisma schema.

## Context

This is the Prisma ORM monorepo. We need to add a new package at `packages/client-generator-rest-api/`. The package must follow the monorepo conventions precisely.

## Monorepo Conventions (verified from codebase)

### Package structure
Every generator package has:
```
packages/<name>/
├── package.json           # name, version 0.0.0, dual CJS/ESM exports, workspace deps
├── tsconfig.json          # { "extends": "../../tsconfig.json", "include": ["./**/*"] }
├── tsconfig.build.json    # { "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json", "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" }, "include": ["src"] }
├── helpers/build.ts       # import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'; import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'; void build(adapterConfig)
└── src/
    ├── index.ts           # main exports (no barrel files)
    └── generator.ts       # implements Generator interface
```

### Generator protocol
- Generators implement `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`:
  ```typescript
  interface Generator {
    readonly name: string
    getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
    generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
  }
  ```
- Entry point uses `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper`:
  ```typescript
  generatorHandler({ onManifest, onGenerate })
  ```
- Key import: `import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'` to resolve output path
- `options.generator.output` is an `EnvValue` — use `parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)` to get the string

### DMMF types (from `@prisma/dmmf`)
```typescript
// Available via options.dmmf.datamodel
type Datamodel = { models: Model[], enums: DatamodelEnum[], types: Model[] }

type Model = {
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
}

type Field = {
  kind: 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  type: string  // e.g., "String", "Int", "Boolean", "DateTime", "Json", "Float", "Decimal", "Bytes", "BigInt", or model name for relations
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}

type DatamodelEnum = {
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]  // { name: string, dbName: string | null }
}
```

### Build system
- `adapterConfig` from `helpers/compile/configs.ts` produces CJS and ESM bundles from `src/index.ts` → `dist/index`
- Package.json exports pattern:
  ```json
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs", 
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": { "types": "./dist/index.d.ts", "default": "./dist/index.js" },
      "import": { "types": "./dist/index.d.mts", "default": "./dist/index.mjs" }
    }
  }
  ```

### CI
New generators in `client-packages` job in `.github/workflows/test-template.yml` — add a step:
```yaml
- run: pnpm run test
  name: 'client-generator-rest-api'
  working-directory: packages/client-generator-rest-api
```

### tsconfig.build.bundle.json paths
Must add: `"@prisma/client-generator-rest-api": ["./packages/client-generator-rest-api/src"]`

### Coding conventions
- kebab-case file names
- No barrel files (import from source file directly)
- Private class fields use `#field` syntax (not `private` keyword)
- No useless code comments (only "why" comments)

## Task

Design a complete implementation plan for:

### 1. Generator package (`packages/client-generator-rest-api/`)

The generator should:
1. Read `options.dmmf.datamodel.models` and `options.dmmf.datamodel.enums`
2. For each model, generate:
   - A TypeScript interface (e.g., `UserInterface`) with fields mapped from DMMF scalar types to TS types
   - CRUD method types: `FindManyArgs`, `FindUniqueArgs`, `CreateArgs`, `UpdateArgs`, `DeleteArgs`
   - Async CRUD methods: `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete` that make HTTP calls
3. Generate a `RestApiClient` class that:
   - Takes a `baseUrl: string` constructor argument
   - Has typed resource accessors (e.g., `client.user.findMany()`)
   - Makes fetch-based HTTP calls (uses built-in `fetch`)
4. Output: one file `client.ts` in the generator output directory

### 2. Generated client structure

The generated output should look like:
```typescript
// Generated REST API client from Prisma schema

export interface User {
  id: number
  email: string
  name: string | null
  createdAt: Date
}

// ...more interfaces

export interface RestApiClientOptions {
  baseUrl: string
  headers?: Record<string, string>
}

class UserResource {
  constructor(private baseUrl: string, private headers: Record<string, string>) {}
  
  async findMany(args?: { skip?: number; take?: number; where?: Partial<User> }): Promise<User[]>
  async findUnique(args: { where: { id: number } }): Promise<User | null>
  async create(args: { data: Omit<User, 'id'> }): Promise<User>
  async update(args: { where: { id: number }; data: Partial<User> }): Promise<User>
  async delete(args: { where: { id: number } }): Promise<User>
}

export class RestApiClient {
  readonly user: UserResource
  // ...
  constructor(options: RestApiClientOptions)
}
```

### 3. HTTP convention

Use REST conventions:
- `findMany` → `GET /users?skip=0&take=10&where=...`
- `findUnique` → `GET /users/:id`
- `create` → `POST /users`
- `update` → `PUT /users/:id`
- `delete` → `DELETE /users/:id`

The ID field should be determined by looking at `field.isId === true` in the DMMF.

### 4. Type mapping

Map DMMF scalar types to TypeScript:
- `String` → `string`
- `Int`, `Float` → `number`
- `Boolean` → `boolean`
- `DateTime` → `Date`
- `Json` → `unknown`
- `Decimal`, `BigInt` → `string` (serialization-safe)
- `Bytes` → `string`
- Enum type → TypeScript enum/union
- Relation fields (`kind: 'object'`) → include them as optional interfaces

### 5. Files to create

List every file to create with exact path and purpose:
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/package.json`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/tsconfig.json`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/tsconfig.build.json`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/helpers/build.ts`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/index.ts`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/generator.ts`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/generate-client.ts` (the main code generation logic)
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/type-mapper.ts` (DMMF type → TS type mapping)
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/model-generator.ts` (per-model interface + resource class code gen)
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/client-generator.ts` (top-level RestApiClient class code gen)

### 6. Files to modify

- `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` — add path alias
- `.github/workflows/test-template.yml` — add CI step

### 7. Tests

Propose a test strategy using Vitest (matching the pattern in other packages). What tests should be written?

Please provide a DETAILED step-by-step implementation plan. Include:
- Exact file contents or pseudocode for each source file
- Which existing packages/functions to import
- Test cases
- Order of implementation steps

- Private class fields use `#field` syntax (not `private` keyword)
- No useless code comments (only "why" comments)

## Task

Design a complete implementation plan for:

### 1. Generator package (`packages/client-generator-rest-api/`)

The generator should:
1. Read `options.dmmf.datamodel.models` and `options.dmmf.datamodel.enums`
2. For each model, generate:
   - A TypeScript interface (e.g., `UserInterface`) with fields mapped from DMMF scalar types to TS types
   - CRUD method types: `FindManyArgs`, `FindUniqueArgs`, `CreateArgs`, `UpdateArgs`, `DeleteArgs`
   - Async CRUD methods: `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete` that make HTTP calls
3. Generate a `RestApiClient` class that:
   - Takes a `baseUrl: string` constructor argument
   - Has typed resource accessors (e.g., `client.user.findMany()`)
   - Makes fetch-based HTTP calls (uses built-in `fetch`)
4. Output: one file `client.ts` in the generator output directory

### 2. Generated client structure

The generated output should look like:
```typescript
// Generated REST API client from Prisma schema

export interface User {
  id: number
  email: string
  name: string | null
  createdAt: Date
}

// ...more interfaces

export interface RestApiClientOptions {
  baseUrl: string
  headers?: Record<string, string>
}

class UserResource {
  constructor(private baseUrl: string, private headers: Record<string, string>) {}
  
  async findMany(args?: { skip?: number; take?: number; where?: Partial<User> }): Promise<User[]>
  async findUnique(args: { where: { id: number } }): Promise<User | null>
  async create(args: { data: Omit<User, 'id'> }): Promise<User>
  async update(args: { where: { id: number }; data: Partial<User> }): Promise<User>
  async delete(args: { where: { id: number } }): Promise<User>
}

export class RestApiClient {
  readonly user: UserResource
  // ...
  constructor(options: RestApiClientOptions)
}
```

### 3. HTTP convention

Use REST conventions:
- `findMany` → `GET /users?skip=0&take=10&where=...`
- `findUnique` → `GET /users/:id`
- `create` → `POST /users`
- `update` → `PUT /users/:id`
- `delete` → `DELETE /users/:id`

The ID field should be determined by looking at `field.isId === true` in the DMMF.

### 4. Type mapping

Map DMMF scalar types to TypeScript:
- `String` → `string`
- `Int`, `Float` → `number`
- `Boolean` → `boolean`
- `DateTime` → `Date`
- `Json` → `unknown`
- `Decimal`, `BigInt` → `string` (serialization-safe)
- `Bytes` → `string`
- Enum type → TypeScript enum/union
- Relation fields (`kind: 'object'`) → include them as optional interfaces

### 5. Files to create

List every file to create with exact path and purpose:
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/package.json`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/tsconfig.json`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/tsconfig.build.json`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/helpers/build.ts`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/index.ts`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/generator.ts`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/generate-client.ts` (the main code generation logic)
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/type-mapper.ts` (DMMF type → TS type mapping)
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/model-generator.ts` (per-model interface + resource class code gen)
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/client-generator.ts` (top-level RestApiClient class code gen)

### 6. Files to modify

- `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` — add path alias
- `.github/workflows/test-template.yml` — add CI step

### 7. Tests

Propose a test strategy using Vitest (matching the pattern in other packages). What tests should be written?

Please provide a DETAILED step-by-step implementation plan. Include:
- Exact file contents or pseudocode for each source file
- Which existing packages/functions to import
- Test cases
- Order of implementation steps

1	import { enginesVersion } from '@prisma/engines-version'
2	import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
3	import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
4	import { getTsconfig } from 'get-tsconfig'
5	import { bold, dim, green } from 'kleur/colors'
6	
7	import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
8	import { inferImportFileExtension, parseGeneratedFileExtension, parseImportFileExtension } from './file-extensions'
9	import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
10	import { inferModuleFormat, parseModuleFormatFromUnknown } from './module-format'
11	import { parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown, RuntimeTargetInternal } from './runtime-targets'
12	
13	const missingOutputErrorMessage = `An output path is required for the \`prisma-client\` generator. Please provide an output path in your schema file:
14	
15	${dim(`generator client {
16	  provider = "prisma-client"`)}
17	${green('  output   = "../src/generated"')}
18	${dim('}')}
19	
20	${bold('Note:')} the output path is relative to the schema directory.
21	`
22	
23	function getOutputPath(config: GeneratorConfig): string {
24	  if (!config.output) {
25	    throw new Error(missingOutputErrorMessage)
26	  }
27	  return parseEnvValue(config.output)
28	}
29	
30	export class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
31	  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'
32	
33	  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
34	    return Promise.resolve({
35	      defaultOutput: getOutputPath(config),
36	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
37	      version: clientVersion,
38	      requiresEngines: [],
39	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
40	    })
41	  }
42	
43	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
44	    const { config } = options.generator
45	    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
46	    const tsconfig = getTsconfig(outputDir)?.config
47	
48	    const target = config.runtime !== undefined ? parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown(config.runtime) : 'nodejs'
49	
50	    const generatedFileExtension =
51	      config.generatedFileExtension !== undefined ? parseGeneratedFileExtension(config.generatedFileExtension) : 'ts'
52	
53	    const importFileExtension =
54	      config.importFileExtension !== undefined
55	        ? parseImportFileExtension(config.importFileExtension)
56	        : inferImportFileExtension({
57	            tsconfig,
58	            generatedFileExtension,
59	            target,
60	          })
61	
62	    const moduleFormat =
63	      config.moduleFormat !== undefined
64	        ? parseModuleFormatFromUnknown(config.moduleFormat)
65	        : inferModuleFormat({
66	            tsconfig,
67	            generatedFileExtension,
68	            importFileExtension,
69	          })
70	
71	    await generateClient({
72	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
73	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
74	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
75	      datasources: options.datasources,
76	      outputDir,
77	      runtimeBase: '@prisma/client/runtime',
78	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
79	      generator: options.generator,
80	      engineVersion: options.version,
81	      clientVersion,
82	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
83	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
84	      target,
85	      generatedFileExtension,
86	      importFileExtension,
87	      moduleFormat,
88	      tsNoCheckPreamble: true, // Set to false only during internal tests
89	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild, target),
90	    })
91	  }
92	}
93	
94	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown, target: RuntimeTargetInternal): 'fast' | 'small' {
95	  if (value === undefined) {
96	    // using the 'small' build for 'vercel-edge' target to fit within their free tier limits
97	    return target === 'vercel-edge' ? 'small' : 'fast'
98	  }
99	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
100	    return value
101	  }
102	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
103	}
104

45	    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
46	    const tsconfig = getTsconfig(outputDir)?.config
47	
48	    const target = config.runtime !== undefined ? parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown(config.runtime) : 'nodejs'
49	
50	    const generatedFileExtension =
51	      config.generatedFileExtension !== undefined ? parseGeneratedFileExtension(config.generatedFileExtension) : 'ts'
52	
53	    const importFileExtension =
54	      config.importFileExtension !== undefined
55	        ? parseImportFileExtension(config.importFileExtension)
56	        : inferImportFileExtension({
57	            tsconfig,
58	            generatedFileExtension,
59	            target,
60	          })
61	
62	    const moduleFormat =
63	      config.moduleFormat !== undefined
64	        ? parseModuleFormatFromUnknown(config.moduleFormat)
65	        : inferModuleFormat({
66	            tsconfig,
67	            generatedFileExtension,
68	            importFileExtension,
69	          })
70	
71	    await generateClient({
72	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
73	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
74	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
75	      datasources: options.datasources,
76	      outputDir,
77	      runtimeBase: '@prisma/client/runtime',
78	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
79	      generator: options.generator,
80	      engineVersion: options.version,
81	      clientVersion,
82	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
83	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
84	      target,
85	      generatedFileExtension,
86	      importFileExtension,
87	      moduleFormat,
88	      tsNoCheckPreamble: true, // Set to false only during internal tests
89	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild, target),
90	    })
91	  }
92	}
93	
94	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown, target: RuntimeTargetInternal): 'fast' | 'small' {
95	  if (value === undefined) {
96	    // using the 'small' build for 'vercel-edge' target to fit within their free tier limits
97	    return target === 'vercel-edge' ? 'small' : 'fast'
98	  }
99	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
100	    return value
101	  }
102	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
103	}

11	type PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {
12	  shouldResolvePrismaClient?: boolean
13	  shouldInstallMissingPackages?: boolean
14	  runtimePath?: string
15	}
16	
17	// const MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING = `\
18	// ${yellow('Warning:')} You did not specify an output path for your \`generator\` in schema.prisma. \
19	// This behavior is deprecated and will no longer be supported in Prisma 7.0.0. To learn more \
20	// visit https://pris.ly/cli/output-path`
21	
22	export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
23	  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
24	
25	  #shouldResolvePrismaClient: boolean
26	  #runtimePath?: string
27	  #cachedPrismaClientPath: string | undefined
28	
29	  constructor({ shouldResolvePrismaClient = true, runtimePath }: PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {}) {
30	    this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient = shouldResolvePrismaClient
31	    this.#runtimePath = runtimePath
32	  }
33	
34	  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
35	    // TODO: warning disabled for now until we fixed issues around custom output paths - see ORM-976
36	    // if (!config.output) {
37	    //   console.warn(MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING)
38	    // }
39	
40	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is true, which is normally the case,
41	    // we find the default output path by resolving the path to the
42	    // `@prisma/client` package. While we resolve the absolute path to the
43	    // package itself, the generator will rewrite it to replace
44	    // `.../@prisma/client` with `.../.prisma/client`, as long as
45	    // `config.isCustomOutput` is false.
46	    //
47	    // If a custom output path is provided, then the default output path doesn't
48	    // matter and we could use a static value here, but that unfortunately
49	    // wouldn't allow us to avoid resolving the client directory and would only
50	    // delay that until the `generate` method call because we copy the runtime
51	    // files if a custom output path is used, and we also copy the WASM modules
52	    // regardles of the output path. Not copying the runtime files is a breaking
53	    // change, so we can only completely get rid of this behaviour in the new
54	    // generator.
55	    //
56	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is false, which is the case when
57	    // using the JSON-RPC compatibility adapter for Prisma Studio, we should use
58	    // a static value here.
59	    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) : '.prisma/client'
60	
61	    return {
62	      defaultOutput,
63	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
64	      version: clientVersion,
65	      requiresEngines: [],
66	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
67	    }
68	  }
69	
70	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
71	    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
72	
73	    await generateClient({
74	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
75	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
76	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
77	      datasources: options.datasources,
78	      outputDir,
79	      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
80	      copyRuntimeSourceMaps: Boolean(process.env.PRISMA_COPY_RUNTIME_SOURCEMAPS),
81	      runtimeSourcePath: await this.#getRuntimePath(options.generator),
82	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
83	      generator: options.generator,
84	      engineVersion: options.version,
85	      clientVersion,
86	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
87	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
88	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild),
89	    })
90	  }
91	
92	  async #getPrismaClientPath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
93	    if (this.#cachedPrismaClientPath) {
94	      return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
95	    }
96	
97	    this.#cachedPrismaClientPath = await resolvePrismaClient(path.dirname(config.sourceFilePath))
98	    return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
99	  }
100	
101	  async #getRuntimePath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
102	    if (this.#runtimePath) {
103	      return this.#runtimePath
104	    }
105	
106	    this.#runtimePath = path.join(await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config), 'runtime')
107	    return this.#runtimePath
108	  }
109	}
110	
111	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown): 'fast' | 'small' {
112	  if (value === undefined) {
113	    return 'fast'
114	  }
115	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
116	    return value
117	  }
118	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
119	}

5	export interface GeneratorConfig {
6	  name: string
7	  output: EnvValue | null
8	  isCustomOutput?: boolean
9	  provider: EnvValue
10	  config: {
11	    /** `output` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.output` */
12	    output?: never
13	    /** `provider` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.provider` */
14	    provider?: never
15	    /** `binaryTargets` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.binaryTargets` */
16	    binaryTargets?: never
17	    /** `previewFeatures` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.previewFeatures` */
18	    previewFeatures?: never
19	  } & {
20	    [key: string]: string | string[] | undefined
21	  }
22	  binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
23	  // TODO why is this not optional?
24	  previewFeatures: string[]
25	  sourceFilePath: string
26	}
27	
28	export interface EnvValue {
29	  fromEnvVar: null | string
30	  value: null | string
31	}
32	
33	export interface BinaryTargetsEnvValue {
34	  fromEnvVar: string | null
35	  value: string
36	  native?: boolean
37	}
38	
39	export type ConnectorType =
40	  | 'mysql'
41	  | 'mongodb'
42	  | 'sqlite'
43	  | 'postgresql'
44	  | 'postgres' // TODO: we could normalize postgres to postgresql this in engines to reduce the complexity?
45	  | 'prisma+postgres' // Note: used for Prisma Postgres, managed by PDP
46	  | 'sqlserver'
47	  | 'cockroachdb'
48	
49	export type ActiveConnectorType = Exclude<ConnectorType, 'postgres' | 'prisma+postgres'>
50	
51	export interface DataSource {
52	  name: string
53	  provider: ConnectorType
54	  // In Rust, this comes from `Connector::provider_name()`
55	  activeProvider: ActiveConnectorType
56	  schemas: string[] | []
57	  sourceFilePath: string
58	}
59	
60	export type BinaryPaths = {
61	  schemaEngine?: { [binaryTarget: string]: string } // key: target, value: path
62	}
63	
64	/** The options passed to the generator implementations */
65	export type GeneratorOptions = {
66	  generator: GeneratorConfig
67	  // TODO: what is otherGenerators for?
68	  otherGenerators: GeneratorConfig[]
69	  schemaPath: string
70	  dmmf: DMMF.Document
71	  datasources: DataSource[]
72	  // TODO deprecate datamodel & rename to schema?
73	  datamodel: string
74	  // TODO is it really always version hash? Feature is unclear.
75	  version: string // version hash
76	  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
77	  noHints?: boolean
78	  allowNoModels?: boolean
79	  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
80	}
81	
82	export type EngineType = 'schemaEngine'
83	
84	export type GeneratorManifest = {
85	  prettyName?: string
86	  defaultOutput?: string
87	  denylists?: {
88	    models?: string[]
89	    fields?: string[]
90	  }
91	  requiresGenerators?: string[]
92	  requiresEngines?: EngineType[]
93	  version?: string
94	  requiresEngineVersion?: string
95	}
96

11	function addSnapshotPathSanitizer({
12	  test,
13	  get,
14	  set,

19	}) {

21	    test: (val) => test(val) && (get(val).includes(__dirname) || get(val).includes('\\')),
22	    serialize(val, config, indentation, depth, refs, printer) {
23	      const newStr = get(val).replaceAll(__dirname, '/project').replaceAll('\\', '/')
24	      return printer(set(val, newStr), config, indentation, depth, refs)
25	    },
26	  })
27	}

297	    expect(omit(generator.options!.generator, ['output'])).toMatchInlineSnapshot(`
298	      {
299	        "binaryTargets": [
300	          {
301	            "fromEnvVar": null,
302	            "native": true,
303	            "value": "NATIVE_BINARY_TARGET",
304	          },
305	        ],
306	        "config": {
307	          "engineType": "client",
308	          "runtime": "workerd",
309	        },
310	        "isCustomOutput": true,
311	        "name": "client",
312	        "previewFeatures": [],
313	        "provider": {
314	          "fromEnvVar": null,
315	          "value": "prisma-client-ts",
316	        },
317	        "sourceFilePath": "/project/workerd.prisma",
318	      }
319	    `)
320	
321	    expect(path.relative(__dirname, parseEnvValue(generator.options!.generator.output!))).toMatchInlineSnapshot(
322	      `"generated"`,
323	    )
324	
325	    await generator.generate()
326	    const clientDir = path.join(__dirname, 'generated')
327	    expect(fs.existsSync(clientDir)).toBe(true)
328	    expect(fs.existsSync(path.join(clientDir, 'client.ts'))).toBe(true)
329	    generator.stop()
330	
331	    function allFiles(dir: string): string[] {
332	      let results: string[] = []
333	      for (const file of fs.readdirSync(dir)) {
334	        const fullPath = path.join(dir, file)
335	        if (fs.statSync(fullPath)?.isDirectory()) {
336	          results = results.concat(allFiles(fullPath))
337	        } else {
338	          results.push(fullPath)
339	        }
340	      }
341	      return results
342	    }
343	
344	    // Check if the adapter property has been generated.
345	    // The adapter property is now part of a union type, so it can be either required or optional (never)
346	    expect(
347	      allFiles(clientDir)
348	        .filter((f) => f.endsWith('.ts'))
349	        .some((f) => /adapter.*runtime\.SqlDriverAdapterFactory/g.test(fs.readFileSync(f, 'utf8'))),
350	    ).toBe(true)
351	  })
352	})

1	name: CI Template job
2	
3	on:
4	  workflow_call:
5	    inputs:
6	      reason:
7	        description: |
8	          Reason why this workflow was called. Could be "buildpulse" for
9	          flaky tests detection or "daily-test" for scheduled full builds,
10	          "CI" for normal pushes/PRs
11	        type: string
12	        required: false
13	        default: CI
14	      jobTimeout:
15	        description: Timeout for the jobs. Default is 35 minutes
16	        type: number
17	        default: 35
18	        required: false
19	      jobsToRun:
20	        description: Jobs to run. See .github/workflows/scripts/detect-jobs-to-run.js script
21	        type: string
22	        default: '-all-'
23	        required: false
24	      pnpmVersion:
25	        description: pnpm package manager version
26	        type: string
27	        default: '10'
28	      engineHash:
29	        description: Customly-built engine hash to use
30	        type: string
31	        required: false
32	      driverAdapterTestJobTimeout:
33	        description: Timeout for driver adapter tests. Default is 60 minutes
34	        type: number
35	        default: 60
36	        required: false
37	    secrets:
38	      BUILDPULSE_ACCESS_KEY_ID:
39	        required: false
40	      BUILDPULSE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY:
41	        required: false
42	      BOT_TOKEN:
43	        required: false
44	
45	env:
46	  HAS_BUILDPULSE_SECRETS: ${{ secrets.BUILDPULSE_ACCESS_KEY_ID != '' && secrets.BUILDPULSE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY != '' }}
47	  PRISMA_TELEMETRY_INFORMATION: 'prisma test.yml'
48	  # To hide "Update available 0.0.0 -> x.x.x"
49	  PRISMA_HIDE_UPDATE_MESSAGE: true
50	  FUNCTIONAL_TEST_OPTIONS: --detectOpenHandles
51	
52	concurrency:
53	  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
54	  # Don't cancel if called from buildpulse workflow
55	  cancel-in-progress: ${{ inputs.reason != 'buildpulse' }}
56	
57	jobs:
58	  #
59	  # Linting
60	  #
61	  lint:
62	    name: Lint
63	    timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.jobTimeout }}
64	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
65	
66	    steps:
67	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
68	
69	      - name: Install & build
70	        uses: ./.github/actions/setup
71	        with:
72	          node-version: '20.19.0'
73	          engine-hash: ${{ inputs.engineHash }}
74	          skip-tsc: false
75	
76	      # https://github.com/actions/toolkit/blob/master/docs/commands.md#problem-matchers
77	      # Matchers are added in setup-node
78	      # https://github.com/actions/setup-node/blob/bacd6b4b3ac3127b28a1e1920c23bf1c2cadbb85/src/main.ts#L54-L60
79	      - name: Disable GitHub annotations from linting
80	        run: |
81	          echo "::remove-matcher owner=eslint-compact::"
82	          echo "::remove-matcher owner=eslint-stylish::"
83	
84	      - name: Run eslint
85	        run: pnpm run lint
86	        working-directory: ./
87	
88	      - name: Run prettier-check
89	        run: pnpm run prettier-check
90	        working-directory: ./
91	
92	      - name: Run check-engines-override
93	        run: pnpm run check-engines-override
94	        working-directory: ./
95	
96	      - name: Run pnpm audit (production dependencies only)
97	        run: pnpm audit --prod
98	
99	  #
100	  # CLIENT (legacy tests without types test)

1	import { ChokidarOptions, watch as createWatcher } from 'chokidar'
2	import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
3	import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
4	import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
5	import glob from 'globby'
6	import path from 'path'
7	
8	import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
9	import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
10	import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
11	import { map } from '../blaze/map'
12	import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
13	import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
14	import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
15	import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
16	import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
17	import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
18	import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'
19	
20	export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
21	export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
22	  name?: string
23	  emitTypes?: boolean
24	  emitMetafile?: boolean
25	  outbase?: never // we don't support this
26	}
27	
28	const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
29	  platform: 'node',
30	  target: 'ES2022',
31	  logLevel: 'error',
32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
33	  metafile: true,
34	} as const
35	
36	/**
37	 * Apply defaults to the original build options
38	 * @param options the original build options
39	 */
40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}
233

236	function writeFileMap(outputDir: string, fileMap: FileMap) {
237	  return Promise.all(
238	    Object.entries(fileMap).map(async ([fileName, content]) => {
239	      const absolutePath = path.join(outputDir, fileName)
240	      // The deletion of the file is necessary, so VSCode
241	      // picks up the changes.
242	      await fs.rm(absolutePath, { recursive: true, force: true })
243	      if (typeof content === 'string' || Buffer.isBuffer(content)) {
244	        // file
245	        await fs.writeFile(absolutePath, content)
246	      } else {
247	        // subdirectory
248	        await fs.mkdir(absolutePath)
249	        await writeFileMap(absolutePath, content)
250	      }
251	    }),
252	  )
253	}

261	    models: [
262	      // Reserved Prisma keywords

264	      'Prisma',
265	      // JavaScript keywords
266	      'async',
267	      'await',
268	      'break',
269	      'case',

272	      'const',
273	      'continue',

288	      'import',

337	/**
338	 * Get all the directories involved in the generation process.
339	 */
340	async function getGenerationDirs({ runtimeBase, outputDir }: GenerateClientOptions) {
341	  const normalizedOutputDir = path.normalize(outputDir)
342	  const normalizedRuntimeBase = pathToPosix(runtimeBase)
343	
344	  const userPackageRoot = await packageUp({ cwd: path.dirname(normalizedOutputDir) })
345	  const userProjectRoot = userPackageRoot ? path.dirname(userPackageRoot) : process.cwd()
346	
347	  return {
348	    runtimeBase: normalizedRuntimeBase,
349	    outputDir: normalizedOutputDir,
350	    projectRoot: userProjectRoot,
351	  }
352	}

354	function getRuntimeNameForTarget(target: RuntimeTargetInternal): RuntimeName {
355	  switch (target) {
356	    case 'nodejs':
357	    case 'deno':
358	      return 'client'
359	
360	    case 'workerd':
361	    case 'vercel-edge':
362	      return 'wasm-compiler-edge'
363	
364	    default:
365	      assertNever(target, 'Unknown runtime target')
366	  }
367	}
368	
369	async function deleteOutputDir(outputDir: string) {
370	  try {
371	    const files = await fs.readdir(outputDir)
372	
373	    if (files.length === 0) {
374	      return
375	    }
376	
377	    if (
378	      !files.includes('client.ts') &&
379	      !files.includes('client.mts') &&
380	      !files.includes('client.cts') &&
381	      !files.includes('client.d.ts') // for legacy js client
382	    ) {
383	      // Make sure users don't accidentally wipe their source code or home directory.
384	      throw new Error(
385	        `${outputDir} exists and is not empty but doesn't look like a generated Prisma Client. ` +
386	          'Please check your output path and remove the existing directory if you indeed want to generate the Prisma Client in that location.',
387	      )
388	    }
389	
390	    await Promise.allSettled(
391	      (
392	        await glob(
393	          [
394	            `${outputDir}/**/*.{js,ts,mts,cts,d.ts}`,
395	            `${outputDir}/**/*.wasm`,
396	            `${outputDir}/*.node`,
397	            `${outputDir}/{query,schema}-engine-*`,
398	            `${outputDir}/package.json`,
399	            `${outputDir}/**/*.prisma`,
400	          ],
401	          {
402	            followSymbolicLinks: false,
403	          },
404	        )
405	      ).map((file) => fs.unlink(file)),
406	    )
407	  } catch (error) {
408	    if ((error as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code !== 'ENOENT') {
409	      throw error
410	    }
411	  }
412	}

4	/**
5	 * Parses an EnvValue (example: Provider) and return the string value
6	 *
7	 * - If there is no env var just return the value
8	 * - If there is an env var it will be resolve and returned.
9	 * - If there is an env var is present but can't be resolved an error will be thrown
10	 */
11	export function parseEnvValue(object: EnvValue) {
12	  if (object.fromEnvVar && object.fromEnvVar != 'null') {
13	    const value = process.env[object.fromEnvVar]
14	    if (!value) {
15	      throw new Error(
16	        `Attempted to load provider value using \`env(${
17	          object.fromEnvVar
18	        })\` but it was not present. Please ensure that ${dim(
19	          object.fromEnvVar,
20	        )} is present in your Environment Variables`,
21	      )
22	    }
23	
24	    return value
25	  }
26	
27	  return object.value as string
28	}
29	
30	/**
31	 * Parses the BinaryTargetsEnvValue and return the string value
32	 *
33	 * - If there is no env var just return the value
34	 * - If there is an env var it will be resolve and returned.
35	 * - If there is an env var is present but can't be resolved an error will be thrown
36	 */
37	export function parseBinaryTargetsEnvValue(object: BinaryTargetsEnvValue): string[] | string {
38	  if (object.fromEnvVar && object.fromEnvVar != 'null') {
39	    const value = process.env[object.fromEnvVar]
40	    if (!value) {
41	      throw new Error(
42	        `Attempted to load binaryTargets value using \`env(${
43	          object.fromEnvVar
44	        })\` but it was not present. Please ensure that ${dim(
45	          object.fromEnvVar,
46	        )} is present in your Environment Variables`,
47	      )
48	    }
49	
50	    // value is a string because it's from env var but need to be parsed
51	    // can be an array like `["darwin"]`
52	    // or a string like `"darwin"`
53	
54	    return JSON.parse(value)
55	  }
56	
57	  return object.value as any
58	}

5	import type { FileMap } from '../generateClient'

7	import { GenericArgsInfo } from '../GenericsArgsInfo'
8	import { createBrowserFile } from './file-generators/BrowserFile'
9	import { createClassFile } from './file-generators/ClassFile'
10	import { createClientFile } from './file-generators/ClientFile'

12	import { createEnumsFile } from './file-generators/EnumsFile'
13	import { createModelFile } from './file-generators/ModelFile'
14	import { createModelsFile } from './file-generators/ModelsFile'
15	import { createPrismaNamespaceBrowserFile } from './file-generators/PrismaNamespaceBrowserFile'
16	import { createPrismaNamespaceFile } from './file-generators/PrismaNamespaceFile'
17	import { GenerateContext } from './GenerateContext'
18	
19	export type RuntimeName = 'wasm-compiler-edge' | 'client' | (string & {})
20	
21	export type TSClientOptions = O.Required<GenerateClientOptions, 'runtimeBase'> & {
22	  /** The name of the runtime bundle to use */
23	  runtimeName: RuntimeName
24	  /** When we are generating an edge-compatible client */
25	  edge: boolean
26	}
27	
28	export class TSClient {
29	  protected readonly dmmf: DMMFHelper
30	  protected readonly genericsInfo: GenericArgsInfo
31	
32	  constructor(protected readonly options: TSClientOptions) {
33	    this.dmmf = new DMMFHelper(options.dmmf)
34	    this.genericsInfo = new GenericArgsInfo(this.dmmf)
35	  }
36	
37	  generateClientFiles(): FileMap {
38	    const context = new GenerateContext({
39	      dmmf: this.dmmf,
40	      genericArgsInfo: this.genericsInfo,
41	      runtimeBase: this.options.runtimeBase,
42	      runtimeImport: `${this.options.runtimeBase}/${this.options.runtimeName}`,
43	      outputFileName: generatedFileNameMapper(this.options.generatedFileExtension),
44	      importFileName: importFileNameMapper(this.options.importFileExtension),
45	      generator: this.options.generator,
46	      provider: this.options.activeProvider,
47	    })
48	
49	    const modelNames = Object.values(context.dmmf.typeAndModelMap)
50	      .filter((model) => context.dmmf.outputTypeMap.model[model.name])
51	      .map((model) => model.name)
52	
53	    const modelsFileMap: FileMap = modelNames.reduce((acc, modelName) => {
54	      acc[context.outputFileName(modelName)] = createModelFile(context, modelName)
55	      return acc
56	    }, {})
57	
58	    return {
59	      [context.outputFileName('client')]: createClientFile(context, this.options),
60	      [context.outputFileName('browser')]: createBrowserFile(context, this.options),
61	      [context.outputFileName('enums')]: createEnumsFile(context),
62	      [context.outputFileName('commonInputTypes')]: createCommonInputTypeFiles(context),
63	      [context.outputFileName('models')]: createModelsFile(context, modelNames),
64	      models: modelsFileMap,
65	      internal: {
66	        [context.outputFileName('prismaNamespace')]: createPrismaNamespaceFile(context, this.options),
67	        [context.outputFileName('prismaNamespaceBrowser')]: createPrismaNamespaceBrowserFile(context),
68	        [context.outputFileName('class')]: createClassFile(context, this.options),
69	      },
70	    }
71	  }
72	}

Design a detailed implementation plan for a new Prisma generator package called `@prisma/client-generator-rest-api` that generates a typed REST API client from a Prisma schema.

## Context

This is the Prisma ORM monorepo. We need to add a new package at `packages/client-generator-rest-api/`. The package must follow the monorepo conventions precisely.

## Monorepo Conventions (verified from codebase)

### Package structure
Every generator package has:
```
packages/<name>/
├── package.json           # name, version 0.0.0, dual CJS/ESM exports, workspace deps
├── tsconfig.json          # { "extends": "../../tsconfig.json", "include": ["./**/*"] }
├── tsconfig.build.json    # { "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json", "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" }, "include": ["src"] }
├── helpers/build.ts       # import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'; import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'; void build(adapterConfig)
└── src/
    ├── index.ts           # main exports (no barrel files)
    └── generator.ts       # implements Generator interface
```

### Generator protocol
- Generators implement `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`:
  ```typescript
  interface Generator {
    readonly name: string
    getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
    generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
  }
  ```
- Entry point uses `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper`:
  ```typescript
  generatorHandler({ onManifest, onGenerate })
  ```
- Key import: `import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'` to resolve output path
- `options.generator.output` is an `EnvValue` — use `parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)` to get the string

### DMMF types (from `@prisma/dmmf`)
```typescript
// Available via options.dmmf.datamodel
type Datamodel = { models: Model[], enums: DatamodelEnum[], types: Model[] }

type Model = {
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
}

type Field = {
  kind: 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  type: string  // e.g., "String", "Int", "Boolean", "DateTime", "Json", "Float", "Decimal", "Bytes", "BigInt", or model name for relations
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}

type DatamodelEnum = {
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]  // { name: string, dbName: string | null }
}
```

### Build system
- `adapterConfig` from `helpers/compile/configs.ts` produces CJS and ESM bundles from `src/index.ts` → `dist/index`
- Package.json exports pattern:
  ```json
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs", 
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": { "types": "./dist/index.d.ts", "default": "./dist/index.js" },
      "import": { "types": "./dist/index.d.mts", "default": "./dist/index.mjs" }
    }
  }
  ```

### CI
New generators in `client-packages` job in `.github/workflows/test-template.yml` — add a step:
```yaml
- run: pnpm run test
  name: 'client-generator-rest-api'
  working-directory: packages/client-generator-rest-api
```

### tsconfig.build.bundle.json paths
Must add: `"@prisma/client-generator-rest-api": ["./packages/client-generator-rest-api/src"]`

### Coding conventions
- kebab-case file names
- No barrel files (import from source file directly)
- Private class fields use `#field` syntax (not `private` keyword)
- No useless code comments (only "why" comments)

## Task

Design a complete implementation plan for:

### 1. Generator package (`packages/client-generator-rest-api/`)

The generator should:
1. Read `options.dmmf.datamodel.models` and `options.dmmf.datamodel.enums`
2. For each model, generate:
   - A TypeScript interface (e.g., `UserInterface`) with fields mapped from DMMF scalar types to TS types
   - CRUD method types: `FindManyArgs`, `FindUniqueArgs`, `CreateArgs`, `UpdateArgs`, `DeleteArgs`
   - Async CRUD methods: `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete` that make HTTP calls
3. Generate a `RestApiClient` class that:
   - Takes a `baseUrl: string` constructor argument
   - Has typed resource accessors (e.g., `client.user.findMany()`)
   - Makes fetch-based HTTP calls (uses built-in `fetch`)
4. Output: one file `client.ts` in the generator output directory

### 2. Generated client structure

The generated output should look like:
```typescript
// Generated REST API client from Prisma schema

export interface User {
  id: number
  email: string
  name: string | null
  createdAt: Date
}

// ...more interfaces

export interface RestApiClientOptions {
  baseUrl: string
  headers?: Record<string, string>
}

class UserResource {
  constructor(private baseUrl: string, private headers: Record<string, string>) {}
  
  async findMany(args?: { skip?: number; take?: number; where?: Partial<User> }): Promise<User[]>
  async findUnique(args: { where: { id: number } }): Promise<User | null>
  async create(args: { data: Omit<User, 'id'> }): Promise<User>
  async update(args: { where: { id: number }; data: Partial<User> }): Promise<User>
  async delete(args: { where: { id: number } }): Promise<User>
}

export class RestApiClient {
  readonly user: UserResource
  // ...
  constructor(options: RestApiClientOptions)
}
```

### 3. HTTP convention

Use REST conventions:
- `findMany` → `GET /users?skip=0&take=10&where=...`
- `findUnique` → `GET /users/:id`
- `create` → `POST /users`
- `update` → `PUT /users/:id`
- `delete` → `DELETE /users/:id`

The ID field should be determined by looking at `field.isId === true` in the DMMF.

### 4. Type mapping

Map DMMF scalar types to TypeScript:
- `String` → `string`
- `Int`, `Float` → `number`
- `Boolean` → `boolean`
- `DateTime` → `Date`
- `Json` → `unknown`
- `Decimal`, `BigInt` → `string` (serialization-safe)
- `Bytes` → `string`
- Enum type → TypeScript enum/union
- Relation fields (`kind: 'object'`) → include them as optional interfaces

### 5. Files to create

List every file to create with exact path and purpose:
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/package.json`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/tsconfig.json`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/tsconfig.build.json`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/helpers/build.ts`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/index.ts`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/generator.ts`
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/generate-client.ts` (the main code generation logic)
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/type-mapper.ts` (DMMF type → TS type mapping)
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/model-generator.ts` (per-model interface + resource class code gen)
- `packages/client-generator-rest-api/src/client-generator.ts` (top-level RestApiClient class code gen)

### 6. Files to modify

- `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` — add path alias
- `.github/workflows/test-template.yml` — add CI step

### 7. Tests

Propose a test strategy using Vitest (matching the pattern in other packages). What tests should be written?

Please provide a DETAILED step-by-step implementation plan. Include:
- Exact file contents or pseudocode for each source file
- Which existing packages/functions to import
- Test cases
- Order of implementation steps

## Plan: `@prisma/client-generator-rest-api`

Create a new package at `packages/client-generator-rest-api/` following the exact monorepo conventions of `client-generator-ts`. **13 files to create, 2 to modify.**

### Architecture

The generator implements the `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator` (in-process, no binary/JSON-RPC needed). It reads `options.dmmf.datamodel` and writes a single `client.ts` file to the configured output directory.

### Source files (`src/`)

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `type-mapper.ts` | Pure DMMF scalar → TS type string (`String`→`string`, `DateTime`→`Date`, `Decimal`/`BigInt`/`Bytes`→`string` for HTTP safety, etc.) |
| `model-generator.ts` | `generateModelInterface()` + `generateResourceClass()` — emits a `UserResource` class with private `#baseUrl`/`#headers` fields and 5 async CRUD methods over `fetch` |
| `client-generator.ts` | `generateRestApiClient()` — emits `RestApiClientOptions` interface + `RestApiClient` class composing all resource accessors |
| `generate-client.ts` | Orchestrates: enums → interfaces → resource classes → client → `fs.writeFile('client.ts')` |
| `generator.ts` | `RestApiGenerator implements Generator` — `getManifest()` + `generate()`, mirrors `PrismaClientTsGenerator` |
| `index.ts` | Exports `RestApiGenerator` and `generateRestApiClientFile` |

### Generated output shape (for `User` model)

```typescript
export type Role = 'ADMIN' | 'USER'           // enums as string unions
export interface User { id: number; email: string; name: string | null }
class UserResource {                           // NOT exported
  async findMany(args?): Promise<User[]>       // GET /users?skip=&take=&where=
  async findUnique(args): Promise<User|null>   // GET /users/:id
  async create(args): Promise<User>            // POST /users
  async update(args): Promise<User>            // PUT /users/:id
  async delete(args): Promise<User>            // DELETE /users/:id
}
export class RestApiClient {
  readonly user: UserResource
  constructor(options: RestApiClientOptions)
}
```

### Config/build (identical to other generator packages)

`package.json` (deps: `@prisma/generator`, `@prisma/dmmf`, `@prisma/internals`), `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.build.json`, `helpers/build.ts` using `adapterConfig` → dual CJS/ESM bundle.

### Tests (Vitest, co-located in `src/`)

- `type-mapper.test.ts` — unit tests for all scalar type mappings and field nullability/list/enum/relation handling
- `model-generator.test.ts` — unit tests for interface/class codegen using inline DMMF fixtures
- `generate-client.test.ts` — integration test writing to `os.tmpdir()`, asserting `client.ts` contents

### Modifications

- `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` line 13: add `"@prisma/client-generator-rest-api": ["./packages/client-generator-rest-api/src"]`
- `.github/workflows/test-template.yml` `client-packages` job: add step `pnpm run test` in `packages/client-generator-rest-api`

### Verification

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm --filter @prisma/client-generator-rest-api build   # → dist/index.{js,mjs,d.ts}
pnpm --filter @prisma/client-generator-rest-api test    # all tests pass
```

| `generator.ts` | `RestApiGenerator implements Generator` — `getManifest()` + `generate()`, mirrors `PrismaClientTsGenerator` exactly |
| `index.ts` | Exports `RestApiGenerator` and `generateRestApiClientFile` |

```typescript
}

class UserResource {                     // not exported; used only by RestApiClient
  async findMany(...)  // GET  /users?skip=&take=&where=
  async findUnique(...)// GET  /users/:id
  async create(...)    // POST /users
  async update(...)    // PUT  /users/:id
  async delete(...)    // DELETE /users/:id
}

export class RestApiClient {
```

### Config / build (identical to all other generator packages)
